product.name: claude-query
product.tagline: Natural language queries against any data source via Claude API
product.author: Sola Samuel / Spearhead Finance Ltd
product.fdeSignal: AI deployment — showing customers how Claude understands their data in 10 minutes
product.vision: A single CLI that connects to any structured data source — PostgreSQL, CSV, JSON, Notion, Airtable — and lets a user query it in plain English. Claude's tool-use API constructs and executes the appropriate query, returning formatted results with the underlying query shown transparently.
successMetrics: An FDE can connect to a customer's database and answer a business question in plain English in under 10 minutes.
Zero destructive queries ever reach a production database (hard safety gate).
--schema-only mode lets the tool run without any production data access.
Same query interface works identically across all five adapters.

epics.title: CLI Foundation & Claude Tool-Use Integration
epics.description: Stand up the TypeScript CLI scaffold with Commander.js and wire in Claude's tool-use API so a natural-language question is turned into a structured query via the execute_query tool.
epics.commit: feat: CLI scaffold with PostgreSQL adapter and Claude tool use

epics.title: Data Source Adapters
epics.description: Implement the DataSourceAdapter interface across all five sources — PostgreSQL, CSV, JSON, Notion, Airtable — each with schema extraction and query execution appropriate to the source.
epics.commit: feat: CSV and JSON adapters; feat: Notion and Airtable adapters

epics.title: Query Safety & Governance
epics.description: Guarantee that destructive queries never execute and that strict-governance environments can run the tool without exposing production data.
epics.commit: feat: query safety validation and schema-only mode

epics.title: Query Modes & Output Formats
epics.description: Provide the operational flags (dry-run, explain, limit) and output formats (table, json, csv, markdown) that make the tool usable in demos and pipelines.
epics.commit: feat: query modes and output formatters

epics.title: Schema Context & Performance
epics.description: Build schema-first context for Claude and manage large schemas via caching and relevant-table selection so queries stay fast and cheap.
epics.commit: feat: schema context construction and caching

epics.title: Session Experience & Distribution
epics.description: Improve repeat-use ergonomics (history, REPL, save) and ship the README and distribution story.
epics.commit: docs: README with full per-source setup guide and examples

features.title: CLI scaffold with Commander.js
features.description: Main command claude-query [question] with global options.

features.title: Claude tool-use call with execute_query tool
features.description: Build the system prompt from schema, send the user question, define the execute_query tool, and handle the tool_use response block.

features.title: DataSourceAdapter interface
features.description: Define the common adapter contract: connect, getSchema, executeQuery, disconnect.

features.title: PostgreSQL adapter
features.description: Connection string; extracts tables, columns/types, FK relationships, row counts, and 3 sample distinct values per column.

features.title: CSV adapter
features.description: Reads file with papaparse; infers column types from first 100 rows; executes queries by filtering the parsed array in-memory.

features.title: JSON adapter
features.description: Handles flat arrays and nested objects; flattens nested paths with dot notation; infers schema from first 10 records.

features.title: Notion adapter
features.description: Database ID; fetches property schema including select option values; execute_query receives a JSON Notion filter object passed to notion.databases.query().

features.title: Airtable adapter
features.description: Base ID + table name; fetches field schema and linked tables; execute_query receives a filterByFormula string.

features.title: Pre-execution SQL safety validation
features.description: Hard gate: parse generated SQL with node-sql-parser; reject anything that is not a SELECT.

features.title: System-prompt safety instruction
features.description: Soft gate: instruct Claude to only generate SELECT statements, never INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DROP/ALTER/CREATE/TRUNCATE.

features.title: --schema-only mode
features.description: Replace execute_query with describe_query so Claude explains the query it would run without executing it; no actual data is ever sent.

features.title: --dry-run mode
features.description: Show the query Claude would run without executing it. Default for first-time connections to an unfamiliar database.

features.title: --explain mode and --limit
features.description: Add a required reasoning field to the tool schema and print it before results; --limit N caps results (default 100).

features.title: Output formatters
features.description: Table (default, cli-table3), JSON, CSV (csv-stringify), and markdown (GFM table).

features.title: Schema-first context construction
features.description: Serialize SchemaContext into a human-readable string for the Claude system prompt; never send raw schema JSON.

features.title: Schema caching and relevant-table selection
features.description: Cache full schema locally keyed by connection-string hash; on subsequent calls ask Claude which tables are relevant and send only those in detail.

features.title: Session history
features.description: Recall the last 10 queries in a session; persist a query history file at ~/.claude-query/history.json.

features.title: Interactive REPL mode and --save
features.description: Ask multiple questions in a session; --save flag exports results to a file.

features.title: README and distribution
features.description: Full per-source setup guide, flag reference, example queries, governance notes, known limitations, and npx distribution.

stories.title: Define the claude-query command and required --source option
stories.iWant: to invoke claude-query [question] --source <connection>
stories.soThat: I can point the tool at any data source from one command
acceptanceCriteria: Running with no --source exits with a clear error.
The positional [question] argument is captured and passed downstream.
--help lists all options.

stories.title: Register global flags
stories.iWant: the flags --dry-run, --schema-only, --limit N, --output, --explain registered
stories.soThat: I can control query behaviour and output
acceptanceCriteria: --output accepts only table|json|csv|markdown and rejects other values.
--limit defaults to 100 and parses as an integer.
Boolean flags default to false.

stories.title: Build the system prompt from schema
stories.iWant: to compose a system prompt from the human-readable schema string plus safety instructions
stories.soThat: Claude reasons over the schema without raw data
acceptanceCriteria: System prompt contains the formatted schema text.
System prompt contains the SELECT-only safety instruction.
No raw row data appears in the system prompt unless explicitly allowed.

stories.title: Define and send the execute_query tool
stories.iWant: to send the execute_query tool definition with query, query_type, reasoning
stories.soThat: Claude returns a structured query rather than free text
acceptanceCriteria: Tool input_schema requires query, query_type, reasoning.
query_type enum is sql|notion_filter|airtable_formula|json_path.
The request includes the user's question as the user message.

stories.title: Handle the tool_use response block
stories.iWant: to parse Claude's tool_use block and extract the query
stories.soThat: I can validate and execute it
acceptanceCriteria: A tool_use block of type execute_query is detected and its input extracted.
A response with no tool_use block produces a clear error.
The extracted query and reasoning are made available downstream.

stories.title: Define DataSourceAdapter interface
stories.iWant: a common adapter contract
stories.soThat: all five sources are interchangeable behind one interface
acceptanceCriteria: Interface declares name, connect, getSchema, executeQuery, disconnect.
getSchema returns a SchemaContext object.
executeQuery returns Row[].

stories.title: Connect to PostgreSQL and extract structural schema
stories.iWant: tables, columns/types, and FK relationships extracted from a connection string
stories.soThat: Claude understands the database structure
acceptanceCriteria: All table names in the public schema are returned.
Each column's name and type is captured.
Foreign key relationships are captured.

stories.title: Capture row counts and sample values
stories.iWant: row counts and 3 sample distinct values per column
stories.soThat: Claude can recognise enum-like fields
acceptanceCriteria: Row count is captured per table.
Up to 3 distinct sample values per column are captured.
TABLESAMPLE SYSTEM is used for large tables to avoid full scans.

stories.title: Execute validated SELECT and return rows
stories.iWant: the generated SELECT executed and rows returned
stories.soThat: I see results for my question
acceptanceCriteria: Only a query that passed safety validation is executed.
Results are returned as Row[].
The connection is closed on disconnect.

stories.title: Parse CSV and infer schema
stories.iWant: column types inferred from the first 100 rows with min/max and 5 sample values
stories.soThat: Claude understands the CSV shape
acceptanceCriteria: Types inferred as string/number/date/boolean.
min/max captured for numeric and date columns.
Row count and 5 sample distinct values per column captured.

stories.title: Execute in-memory filter on CSV
stories.iWant: queries executed by filtering the parsed array in memory
stories.soThat: I can query a CSV with no database
acceptanceCriteria: Claude generates a filter using the json_path query_type.
The filter is applied to the parsed array.
Matching rows are returned as Row[].

stories.title: Infer JSON schema from first 10 records
stories.iWant: schema inferred from the first 10 records of a flat or nested JSON
stories.soThat: Claude understands the JSON shape
acceptanceCriteria: Flat arrays of objects are supported.
Nested objects are supported.
Nested paths are flattened with dot notation.

stories.title: Execute json_path filter on JSON
stories.iWant: queries executed against the in-memory JSON array
stories.soThat: I can query a JSON file
acceptanceCriteria: json_path query_type is used.
Dot-notation paths resolve against nested objects.
Matching records are returned as Row[].

stories.title: Fetch Notion database property schema
stories.iWant: property names/types and select option values fetched from a database ID
stories.soThat: Claude can build a valid Notion filter
acceptanceCriteria: Property names and types (title/text/number/select/date/relation) captured.
All option values of select/multi-select properties included.
Database name captured.

stories.title: Execute Notion filter object
stories.iWant: Claude's JSON Notion filter object passed to databases.query()
stories.soThat: I get matching pages back
acceptanceCriteria: query_type is notion_filter.
The filter/sorts object is passed directly to notion.databases.query().
Returned pages are mapped to Row[].

stories.title: Fetch Airtable field schema
stories.iWant: field names/types and linked table names fetched for a base + table
stories.soThat: Claude can build a valid formula
acceptanceCriteria: Field names and types captured.
Linked table names captured.
Select field options captured.

stories.title: Execute Airtable filterByFormula
stories.iWant: Claude's filterByFormula string applied to the table
stories.soThat: I get matching records back
acceptanceCriteria: query_type is airtable_formula.
The formula string is passed as filterByFormula.
Returned records are mapped to Row[].

stories.title: Reject non-SELECT SQL before execution
stories.iWant: any non-SELECT statement rejected before execution
stories.soThat: destructive queries never run
acceptanceCriteria: SQL is parsed with node-sql-parser.
INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DROP/ALTER/CREATE/TRUNCATE are rejected with a clear error.
A valid SELECT passes.

stories.title: Reject multi-statement and obfuscated SQL
stories.iWant: stacked or comment-obfuscated statements rejected
stories.soThat: the gate cannot be bypassed
acceptanceCriteria: Multiple statements separated by ; are rejected.
A SELECT with a trailing DELETE is rejected.
Parse failure results in rejection, not execution.

stories.title: Include SELECT-only instruction in system prompt
stories.iWant: the system prompt to forbid non-SELECT statements
stories.soThat: Claude rarely attempts a destructive query (soft gate)
acceptanceCriteria: System prompt names the forbidden statement types explicitly.
Instruction is present for every SQL-source request.

stories.title: Swap execute_query for describe_query in schema-only mode
stories.iWant: --schema-only to use a describe_query tool that never executes
stories.soThat: no production data is touched
acceptanceCriteria: describe_query tool replaces execute_query when --schema-only is set.
Claude returns the query it would run plus an explanation.
No adapter executeQuery call is made.

stories.title: Guarantee no data sent in schema-only mode
stories.iWant: assurance that only schema (never rows) is sent in schema-only mode
stories.soThat: the tool is safe under strict data governance
acceptanceCriteria: Sample-value capture is suppressed or governance-approved in schema-only mode.
Outbound payload contains no row data.
Mode is reflected in console output.

stories.title: Dry-run shows query without executing
stories.iWant: --dry-run to print the constructed query and stop
stories.soThat: I can review before touching production
acceptanceCriteria: The constructed query is printed.
No executeQuery call is made.
Exit is clean with no results table.

stories.title: Explain prints reasoning before results
stories.iWant: --explain to print Claude's reasoning before the results
stories.soThat: I understand why the query answers my question
acceptanceCriteria: reasoning is a required field on the tool schema.
Reasoning text is printed before the results.
Without --explain, reasoning is captured but not printed.

stories.title: Limit caps result rows
stories.iWant: --limit N to cap returned rows regardless of the query
stories.soThat: large result sets stay manageable
acceptanceCriteria: Default cap is 100 rows.
Results are truncated to N rows.
Truncation is indicated to the user.

stories.title: Table output (default)
stories.iWant: results pretty-printed as an ASCII table
stories.soThat: results are readable in the terminal
acceptanceCriteria: cli-table3 renders headers and rows.
Table is the default when --output is omitted.
Empty result sets render a clear 'no rows' message.

stories.title: JSON output
stories.iWant: --output json to emit a raw JSON array
stories.soThat: I can pipe results to other tools
acceptanceCriteria: Output is a valid JSON array of objects.
No table formatting is printed.
Output parses with JSON.parse.

stories.title: CSV output
stories.iWant: --output csv to emit CSV with headers
stories.soThat: I can open results in a spreadsheet
acceptanceCriteria: csv-stringify produces a header row.
Values containing commas/quotes are escaped.
Row count matches the result set (after limit).

stories.title: Markdown output
stories.iWant: --output markdown to emit a GFM table
stories.soThat: I can paste results into docs
acceptanceCriteria: Output is a valid GFM table with header separator row.
Column alignment row is present.
Table renders correctly when pasted into a markdown viewer.

stories.title: Serialize SchemaContext to readable text
stories.iWant: SchemaContext rendered as human-readable text, not raw JSON
stories.soThat: Claude reasons over it more naturally
acceptanceCriteria: Output is formatted prose/tabular text, not JSON.
Tables, columns, types and relationships are all represented.
Same renderer is used across adapters.

stories.title: Cache full schema by connection hash
stories.iWant: the full schema cached locally keyed by a connection-string hash
stories.soThat: repeat queries in a session avoid re-extraction
acceptanceCriteria: Cache key is a hash of the connection string.
A cache hit skips re-extraction.
Cache is scoped to the session.

stories.title: Send only relevant tables on subsequent calls
stories.iWant: Claude to name relevant_tables so only those are sent in detail
stories.soThat: large schemas stay cheap and fast
acceptanceCriteria: Tool schema includes a relevant_tables field.
First call sends full schema; later calls send only named tables in detail.
Behaviour degrades gracefully for small schemas.

stories.title: Recall last 10 queries in session
stories.iWant: the last 10 queries recalled within a session
stories.soThat: I can re-run or reference recent queries
acceptanceCriteria: Up to 10 recent queries are retained.
Recall lists question and constructed query.
Order is most-recent-first.

stories.title: Persist history file
stories.iWant: a history file at ~/.claude-query/history.json
stories.soThat: history survives across sessions
acceptanceCriteria: History is written to ~/.claude-query/history.json.
The directory is created if missing.
Malformed history files are handled without crashing.

stories.title: Interactive REPL mode
stories.iWant: to ask multiple questions in one session
stories.soThat: I avoid re-connecting per question
acceptanceCriteria: REPL accepts repeated questions until exit.
Schema cache is reused across questions.
A clear exit command ends the session.

stories.title: Save results with --save
stories.iWant: --save to export the result set to a file
stories.soThat: I can keep query outputs
acceptanceCriteria: --save writes results in the chosen --output format.
A file path is reported on success.
Existing-file behaviour is defined (overwrite or error).

stories.title: Per-source README setup guide
stories.iWant: connection formats and examples for each adapter plus a flag reference
stories.soThat: I can get started quickly
acceptanceCriteria: Installation (npx and global) documented.
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY setup documented.
Per-adapter connection format, 5 example queries, and flag reference included.

stories.title: Document governance mode and known limitations
stories.iWant: the --schema-only governance angle and known limitations documented
stories.soThat: I can assess fit for a regulated environment
acceptanceCriteria: --schema-only explained for data governance.
Known limitations listed (no cross-adapter JOINs, Notion relation queries limited to IDs).
Distribution channels noted.
